title: CI publish-gate linter (no CONTROLLED/NC; rights cited; tier-consistent)
context: The CI publish-gate linter is the automated enforcement layer that blocks any record violating the access-tier rules from being merged into the catalog. It is distinct from the schema validator (validator-004): where the validator checks structural validity, this linter checks semantic business rules. Key rules: CONTROLLED and NON-COMMERCIAL accessTier values must never appear in a published record (unless a governance-decision reference per policy-nc-034 is attached); every rights boolean must have a fieldProvenance entry; OPEN-tier records may not carry duaRequired:true; dead/unresolvable citations fail. Critically, the linter must NOT reject a record for license.redistributionAllowed:false — index != redistribute; GENIE is a valid REGISTERED-OPEN index entry. Depends on schema-001 and validator-004.
objective: Implement a CI publish-gate linter that blocks merging of any catalog record with CONTROLLED/NON-COMMERCIAL access tiers, uncited rights booleans, or OPEN records with duaRequired:true — while correctly passing REGISTERED-OPEN records with redistributionAllowed:false — with synthetic golden fixtures proving each rule.
acceptanceCriteria: CI blocks merge of any catalog record with accessTier: CONTROLLED or accessTier: NON-COMMERCIAL, unless a governance-decision reference is attached (per policy-nc-034); the block error message names the specific violation.
CI blocks merge of any record with a rights boolean (permitsReuse, permitsDerivatives, commercialUseAllowed, redistributionAllowed, attributionRequired, shareAlike) lacking a fieldProvenance entry.
CI blocks merge of any OPEN-tier record with accessTerms.duaRequired: true.
CI blocks merge of any record with an unresolvable fieldProvenance.sourceUrl or license.url (mocked in CI to avoid network calls in the test suite).
CI does NOT reject a record for license.redistributionAllowed: false; a golden fixture asserts a REGISTERED-OPEN record (e.g. GENIE-like) with redistributionAllowed:false passes the linter.
All access-tier consistency rules are encoded as tests with synthetic fixtures (no real cohort data); pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; MIT-licensed; DCO signed-off.
output: A committed CI publish-gate linter (MIT) that blocks access-tier, rights-citation, and tier-consistency violations while correctly allowing redistributionAllowed:false records, with synthetic golden fixtures and CI integration.
